page: Microsoft Azure DS4_v2 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor clocked at 2.6 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 35 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache, with support for AVX, AVX-512, AES, and FMA instructions. The instance is equipped with 28.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.5 GB per core, and includes 57 GB of local storage.

Benchmark data reveals a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the bottom 25% tier. Similarly, single-threaded operations like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression are categorized as weak. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core score, and PassMark CPU score. Memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks also registers in the middle 50% tier, though the overall PassMark memory score remains in the bottom 25%.

For application workloads, the DS4_v2 delivers average performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput. It also handles small-model LLM inference at average speeds, though performance degrades to weak for prompt processing on medium 7B models. Given the dedicated CPU allocation and balanced memory-to-core ratio, this instance is suited for multi-threaded general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, and moderate database operations, offering a standard resource density profile without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware.
description: Microsoft Azure DS4_v2 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor clocked at 2.6 GHz. It features 8 dedicated physical cores, a 35 MB L3 cache, 28.0 GB of system memory, and 57 GB of local storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance supports AVX-512 and AES instructions. Performance benchmarks indicate a tradeoff: single-core CPU execution, single-threaded compression, and memory scores fall into the bottom 25% tier, whereas multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations achieve average, middle-tier results. The instance is suited for multi-threaded general-purpose applications, static web serving, Redis deployments, and moderate database workloads that benefit from dedicated CPU resources without requiring high-performance single-core execution or GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DS4_v2 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 28 GB RAM, and 57 GB local storage, delivering average multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DS4_v2 is an 8-core Intel Xeon virtual machine with 28 GB RAM and 57 GB local storage, suited for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An 8-core x86_64 virtual machine with dedicated Intel Xeon processors, balanced memory, and local storage for multi-threaded general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL physical cores at 2.6 GHz
28.0 GB system memory and 57 GB local storage
Average multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Weak single-core and single-threaded execution profiles
Suited for static web serving, Redis, and moderate database workloads
